articles.title: Iran war day 104: Iran attacks US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan, closes Strait of Hormuz after US strikes
articles.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps says it hit 18 targets at US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan in retaliation for renewed US strikes. Iran's joint military command declared the Strait of Hormuz closed to oil tankers and commercial ships, saying any vessel attempting passage will be fired on.

articles.title: Trump vows to hit Iran 'very hard tonight', threatens to seize Kharg Island and take over Iran's oil and gas sectors
articles.summary: As the US and Iran traded strikes for a second day, President Trump vowed a 'bigger' and 'more powerful' round of strikes and said the US would seize Iran's oil export hub Kharg Island and other oil infrastructure. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth confirmed strikes on 'key facilities'; explosions were reported in Sirik, Bandar Abbas, Qeshm and Kharg Island. Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Ghalibaf warned attacks on infrastructure would create an 'endless quagmire'.

articles.title: Water cut off for thousands of Iranians after US strikes on water facilities
articles.summary: Iranian state media says some 20,000 residents around Jask, Sirik and Qeshm Island lost access to safe drinking water after US strikes hit water infrastructure, with temperatures in the region between 45 and 50C. Al Jazeera analysis calls the targeting of water facilities a significant escalation.

articles.title: Three Indian sailors killed in US strike on tanker MT Settebello off Oman; India lodges protest
articles.summary: Three Indian sailors were confirmed dead after a US military strike on the Palau-flagged tanker MT Settebello, accused of transporting Iranian oil, off the coast of Oman. India lodged a formal protest with Washington.

articles.title: Iran says US strikes render ceasefire 'practically meaningless' as projectile hits cargo barge off Oman
articles.summary: Iran's foreign ministry said renewed US strikes render the April ceasefire 'practically meaningless'. Tehran reported a US projectile hit an Iranian cargo barge off Oman, with the crew rescued.

articles.title: IDF poised to launch offensive against Hezbollah in Nabatieh after secret Litani River crossing
articles.summary: An IDF official says the military is set to launch an offensive against Hezbollah in the southern Lebanon city of Nabatieh. The 36th Division is operating on the outskirts of Arnoun after clandestinely building a bridge over the Litani River in an unsurveilled valley, allowing armored vehicles to advance northward undetected.

articles.title: Major Haredi protests against draft-evader arrests jam central Israel; man seriously hurt at Geha Junction
articles.summary: Thousands of Haredi protesters brought traffic on Israel's central highways to a standstill in demonstrations against the arrest of draft evaders. A 93-year-old man was injured when a driver slammed into a crowd near Bnei Brak and is in moderate-to-serious condition.

articles.title: EU foreign policy chief Kallas discusses war escalation with Iran's Araghchi, urges diplomatic off-ramp
articles.summary: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said she discussed the latest escalation with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and reiterated the need for a diplomatic way out of the conflict.

articles.title: Russia and China call for 'restraint' in Iran war after new US-Iran strikes
articles.summary: Russia and China urged restraint after Washington and Tehran traded new strikes in the worst escalation since the April 8 ceasefire.

articles.title: Arab Group at UN condemns Iranian attacks on Gulf states and Israeli actions across the region
articles.summary: The Arab Group at the United Nations condemned both Iranian missile attacks on Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan and Israeli military actions across the Middle East, calling for de-escalation.

articles.title: Hamas delegation holds talks with Russian deputy foreign minister on Gaza ceasefire efforts
articles.summary: A Hamas delegation met Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Georgy Borisenko in Moscow on Wednesday for talks on Gaza ceasefire negotiations and international efforts to prevent a renewed outbreak of war.

articles.title: Opinion: Arab states condemned Israel publicly, but quietly moved on from Gaza
articles.summary: Opinion piece arguing that Arab governments' public condemnations of Israel over Gaza have not been matched by substantive policy changes, with normalization tracks quietly maintained.
